title: The Gilgo Beach Killer Got Life. Families Finally Spoke.
script: 8 murders. Finally a sentence! Rex Heuermann — a Long Island architect — had a double life for decades. A normal-looking family man by day, a predator by night. He strangled 8 women and disposed of their bodies along Gilgo Beach on Long Island's south shore. Their remains sat undiscovered for years. Then DNA and cell-tower data cracked the case, and in 2023, Heuermann was arrested in broad daylight. On June 17, 2026, Judge Timothy Mazzei sentenced him to consecutive life terms with no possibility of parole. 'A disgusting and small man,' the judge called him. The victims' families, who had waited decades for this moment, erupted in applause in the courtroom. Eight women. Eight families. Decades of waiting. Finally over. So: how many Gilgo Beach victims are still unidentified?
